title: UI & Menu Polish: Preview, Controls, Scroll Fixes
content:
## Overview

Commit `b7b116ef` delivered four coordinated UI/UX cleanup items: upgrading the landing-page snake preview to match the real game's visual quality, removing two vestigial bot-control surfaces from the game screen, and preventing arrow keys from scrolling the page during play.

---

## Task 1 â€” Landing-page preview parity (`src/menu-demo.ts`, `index.html`, `src/style.css`)

### Head & tail graphics

The `MenuDemo` renderer previously drew the snake as plain identical blocks. It now ports the same rendering pipeline used by the main game:

- **`drawPrism(corners, bh, top, right, left)`** â€” shared extrusion helper extracted so both standard body blocks and the tapered tail reuse the same drawing path.
- **`drawBlock`** delegates to `drawPrism` and accepts a `heightScale = 1` parameter.
- **`drawSnakeHead(gx, gy, colors, dir)`** â€” renders a taller block (`HEAD_HEIGHT_SCALE = 1.6`) with two eyes on the leading face.
- **`drawSnakeTail(gx, gy, colors, awayDir)`** + **`tailDirection(snake)`** + **`vectorToDirection(v)`** â€” tapered tail prism pointing in the trailing direction.
- Constants: `HEAD_HEIGHT_SCALE = 1.6`, `TAIL_HEIGHT_SCALE = 0.7`.

Segment classification in `draw()`: `index === 0 â†’ head`, `index === length-1 â†’ tail`, otherwise `body`. A length-1 snake is treated as head-only (no tail). Color tuples match the main game's P1 palette: head `['#4ade80','#22c55e','#16a34a']`, body `['#22c55e','#16a34a','#15803d']`, food `['#ef4444','#dc2626','#b91c1c']`.

### Iron Snake / classic board (random per page load)

- `generateIronSnakeBoard` is imported from `./iron-snake`; a `boardMask: Uint8Array | null` field tracks the current board shape.
- An `onBoard(x, y)` helper checks rectangle bounds and, when `boardMask` is set, `boardMask[y*gridSize+x] === 1`. `inBounds` delegates to `onBoard`, so collision detection, bot pathfinding, and food spawning all respect the irregular shape automatically.
- **Board type is decided once per page load**, not per life. A 50/50 per-life split was tried but almost always showed the classic board in screenshots because the snake survives much longer on the roomy rectangle. Locking the style per visit means a given page load consistently showcases one style; reloading shows the other.
- `resetSnake()` generates a fresh board of the committed type on every life. If `generateIronSnakeBoard` returns null, the demo falls back to classic (`boardMask = null`).
- `DEMO_GRID_SIZE` is bumped to ~14 to give irregular shapes enough room (Iron Snake `MIN_GRID` is 12).
- The snake spawns at the on-board cell nearest center (center may be void on an irregular board).
- `spawnFood()` only considers on-board empty cells.
- `drawGroundPlane`, `drawGridLines`, and `drawGroundBorder` all have masked branches that skip off-board cells and trace the true irregular outline for Iron boards, with full-rectangle branches retained for classic.

### Random bot + corner label

- On every life reset, `resetSnake()` picks a random bot from `AVAILABLE_BOTS` (replaces the fixed `DEFAULT_BOT_ID`).
- A `<div id="menu-demo-bot-label" class="menu-demo-bot-label">` element was added to `index.html` inside `.menu-hero`.
- Styled in `src/style.css` as a small translucent dark pill with a green border, absolutely positioned in the top-left corner of the hero container â€” rendered in the DOM rather than on the canvas so text is crisp at all resolutions and accessible, with no text-drawing pass added to the animation loop.
- `MenuDemo` queries the element once in the constructor and updates its text (e.g. `Bot: <name>`) in `resetSnake()`.

---

## Task 2 â€” Remove the "Disable Bot" button (`index.html`, `src/main.ts`)

The `#toggle-bot` button and its `B` keyboard shortcut were vestigial: bot play moved to the pre-game Solo Bot menu panel, making mid-session toggling confusing and unused.

**Removed:**
- `#toggle-bot` button element from `index.html`; "Press B to toggle bot" from the controls hint text.
- Click listener, `B`-key branch, and `toggleBot()` method from `src/main.ts`.
- The null-guard in `updateBotUI()` that referenced the deleted button element (leaving it would have silently stopped the bot status display from updating).
- `toggleBot` lookup and its `.hidden` toggle from `updateModeUI()`; "Press B to toggle bot" from the single-player controls string.

**Kept intact:** `botEnabled` flag, `botEverActive` leaderboard-disqualification flag, the Solo Bot launch path that sets `botEnabled = true`, human-input suppression that reads the flag, and the leaderboard check that uses `botEverActive`.

---

## Task 3 â€” Remove mid-game bot switching (`index.html`, `src/main.ts`, `src/style.css`)

The `#game-bot-selection` dropdown on the live game screen allowed swapping the driving bot mid-game, inconsistent with the pre-game bot selection panels.

**Removed:**
- `#game-bot-selection` block (`#game-bot-select` + `#game-bot-description`) from `index.html`.
- `gameSelect` from the `botSelectors` array in `setupBotSelectors()`; `'game-bot-description'` from `bot1Ids` in `updateBotDescriptions()`.
- `gameBotSelection` lookup and its `.hidden` toggle from `updateModeUI()`.
- Dead `.bot-selection` CSS rules and their responsive variant from `src/style.css`.

Bot selection is now exclusively a pre-game action via the Solo Bot and Bot vs. Bot menu panels. The existing `#bot-status` display on the game screen still shows the active bot's name for Solo Bot runs.

---

## Task 4 â€” Arrow keys scroll the page (`src/main.ts`)

On short or narrow viewports where the page overflows, arrow keys moved the snake and scrolled the page simultaneously.

**Fix:** At the top of `handleKeyPress`, immediately after the existing guard that exempts `INPUT`/`TEXTAREA`/`SELECT` elements, an explicit `e.preventDefault()` is called for `ArrowUp`, `ArrowDown`, `ArrowLeft`, and `ArrowRight`. Prevention is unconditional across all game states (not just when a game is active) to avoid any simultaneous scroll. WASD is unaffected. Typing in forms is unaffected by the pre-existing text-field guard.

---

## Bot loop-guard fix (`src/bots/loop-guard.ts`)

A related fix corrected the escape state machine in the loop-guard shared by bots:

- **Problem:** The escape timer was reset to its full value on every tick that a valid escape move existed, so it could never count down to zero. The bot would enter escape mode and stay there permanently.
- **Fix:** Escape is now started only on a *fresh* loop detection when no escape is already in progress. The countdown runs for a fixed number of ticks. When it reaches zero, the visit history is cleared (preventing immediate re-detection of the same stale loop) and the bot's own direction is restored.
- **Decision â€” clear visit history on escape exit:** Without clearing, the bot would immediately re-detect the same stale positions and restart escape mode, effectively staying in it indefinitely via a different path. Clearing gives the bot a clean slate to resume its own strategy.

---
